paper.title: A neuromorphic continuous soil monitoring system for precision irrigation
paper.abstract: Sensory processing at the edge requires ultra-low power stand-alone computing technologies. This is particularly true for modern agriculture and precision irrigation systems which aim to optimize water usage by monitoring key environmental observables continuously using distributed efficient embedded processing elements. Neuromorphic processing systems are emerging as a promising technology for extreme edge-computing applications that need to run on resource-constrained hardware. As such, they a
